context: Tests whether the agent implements idempotency (checking for existing invoice before creating), snapshots buyer/seller data on creation, correctly determines EU VAT invoice type, makes QuickBooks sync non-blocking with error handling, stores QB document ID in metadata, uses node-quickbooks package, and uses a billing@ sender address for invoice emails.

checklist.name: Idempotency check
checklist.description: generateAndSendInvoice checks for an existing non-void invoice for the same order_id before creating a new one, and returns the existing invoice if found

checklist.name: Seller data snapshot
checklist.description: The invoice record creation includes seller_name, seller_address, and seller_tax_id fields populated from a company/settings source (not left null or omitted)

checklist.name: Buyer data snapshot
checklist.description: The invoice record creation includes buyer_name, buyer_address, and buyer_tax_id (VAT number) fields populated from the customer/order source

checklist.name: EU VAT type detection
checklist.description: Invoice type is determined by checking whether the customer's country is in a list of EU countries AND whether the seller is VAT-registered (not just one condition alone)

checklist.name: EU_COUNTRIES constant
checklist.description: A named constant (e.g. EU_COUNTRIES) holds the array of EU country codes used for VAT detection

checklist.name: Non-blocking accounting sync
checklist.description: The call to pushToAccounting (or equivalent) uses .catch() or try/catch such that a rejection/error does NOT propagate and reject the overall generateAndSendInvoice promise

checklist.name: Sync failure logged
checklist.description: When the accounting sync fails, the error is logged (console.error or equivalent) with at least the invoice ID and error message — it is not silently swallowed

checklist.name: node-quickbooks package
checklist.description: accounting-sync.js imports QuickBooks from 'node-quickbooks' (not a different QuickBooks client or a generic HTTP client)

checklist.name: QB doc ID stored in metadata
checklist.description: After a successful QuickBooks sync, the QuickBooks invoice ID (from the API response) is saved into the invoice's metadata field (e.g. as qb_invoice_id)

checklist.name: Billing sender address
checklist.description: The invoice email sender (From or Reply-To) is configured with a 'billing@' address — NOT a 'noreply@' address

checklist.name: Invoice immutability noted
checklist.description: The code or comments indicate that sent invoices should not be edited and that corrections require a credit note (e.g. a comment, a guard, or a test case)
